jokes.text: By giving them fresh apple slices.
jokes.setup: Threatening interrogation tactics - 'We have ways of making people talk'
jokes.punchline: Wholesome snack offering instead of torture
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection from threatening interrogation to wholesome parenting. Economy is excellent - three words completely subvert expectation.

jokes.text: Do you know how much our COBRA payments are? And he's the only one that knows the combination to the candy safe. The worst part is being able to see the candy!
jokes.setup: Question about wanting Tracy back
jokes.punchline: Escalating from practical concerns to absurd candy safe tragedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect workplace comedy escalation - from reasonable (COBRA) to absurd (candy safe) with genuine pathos in the final line.

jokes.text: That's ironic, because next week's topic is fascism.
jokes.setup: Threat to ban History Club for not cooperating
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the fascist irony while staying in character as history nerd
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Walter stays true to his nerdy nature while delivering perfect ironic observation about the situation.

jokes.text: Edward James Almost... who is an Edward James Olmos look-alike that Tracy is friends with...
jokes.setup: Listing Tracy's close contacts
jokes.punchline: Celebrity name becomes different person entirely through wordplay
jokes.explanation: Classic 30 Rock absurdist wordplay - takes celebrity name and creates entirely new character through clever manipulation.

jokes.text: I'm going to go ahead and assume that Bastille is a stripper. As well you should.
jokes.setup: Kenneth mentions Tracy calls on Bastille Day
jokes.punchline: Liz assumes it's a stripper, Kenneth confirms
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moments - Liz's cynical assumption and Kenneth's matter-of-fact confirmation create the comedy.

jokes.text: Has it really been a year since we were all at the Waldorf eating that cake made out of lizard meal? that we saw Tracy eating from.
jokes.setup: Mentioning Tracy's lizard's birthday
jokes.punchline: Escalating to absurd lizard meal cake at fancy hotel
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from pet birthday to elaborate Waldorf party with lizard meal cake - pure 30 Rock absurdism.

jokes.text: Listen to me, I sound like Cagney and Lacey, but without the slutty clothes.
jokes.setup: Jack describing his detective work
jokes.punchline: Self-aware reference to 80s cop show with judgmental fashion note
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment - pop culture reference combined with his particular brand of judgment and vanity.

jokes.text: We produce more failed pilots than the French Air Force.
jokes.setup: Explaining TV industry business model
jokes.punchline: Clever pun connecting TV pilots to airplane pilots with French military joke
jokes.explanation: Exceptional wordplay that works on multiple levels - TV pilots/airplane pilots, plus French military surrender stereotype. Perfectly economical.

jokes.text: $15 million, so far, developing a show called 'Who Nose?' About an investigative reporter who can't smell and has to get the story using only his eyes, ears and other senses.
jokes.setup: Discussing failed pilot development costs
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun-based show concept with redundant premise explanation
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist TV industry satire with terrible pun title. The redundant explanation ('using only his eyes, ears and other senses') adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: You underestimated me, Congressman, because I can't smell. But you made one mistake: You let me see the documents.
jokes.setup: Sample dialogue from the terrible pilot show
jokes.punchline: Taking the absurd premise completely seriously with dramatic delivery
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that commits to the absurd premise with serious dramatic delivery. Shows rather than tells how bad the pilot would be.

jokes.text: Your father may be gone, but before he died he programmed me to take his place... No! Shut it down! This is terrible.
jokes.setup: Sample from another terrible pilot about robot father replacement
jokes.punchline: Jack's immediate horrified reaction and cancellation
jokes.explanation: Great structure - sets up awful concept and Jack's immediate executive decision. The robot father premise is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: That's what your wife told me in the shower this morning.
jokes.setup: Pete asserting his role as producer
jokes.punchline: Crude 'your wife' comeback in unexpected context
jokes.explanation: Lazy 'your wife' joke that relies on crude shock value rather than wit. Sets up running gag but not clever.

jokes.text: All right, well, we're all having fun, and it's important that we all be able to laugh together. We're not laughing with you, we're laughing at you, you bald bitch!
jokes.setup: Pete trying to defuse tension with diplomatic language
jokes.punchline: Immediate escalation to direct insult, undermining his peace-making
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure - Pete's diplomatic attempt immediately undermined. The contrast between his peacemaking and the harsh response works well.

jokes.text: Yes, Reggie, I am bald. I lost my hair at a very early age when I hit that gypsy's kid with my car, but I am still your boss...
jokes.setup: Pete acknowledging the bald insult
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of horrific car accident as hair loss explanation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - casual mention of hitting a child becomes explanation for baldness. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Listen up, fives, a ten is speaking.
jokes.setup: Jenna addressing the writers
jokes.punchline: Rating everyone as 5s while claiming to be a 10
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna character moment - maximum narcissism in minimum words. Became iconic line for the character's vanity.

jokes.text: Let's just go to soccer practice now! I mean right now! Yes! It's 4:00 in the morning! Let's do it! We own this town!
jokes.setup: JennaBabies dolls being recalled for cocaine smuggling
jokes.punchline: Children's toy commercial dialogue that sounds like drug-fueled mania
jokes.explanation: Dark brilliance - children's toy commercial that inadvertently promotes drug behavior. The 4 AM soccer practice detail sells the absurdity.

jokes.text: It's called 'Take My Hand.' It's kind of an artsy character study about a young woman who has a lot of holes drilled into her head by a deranged veterinarian named Slaughterface.
jokes.setup: Jenna describing her 'artsy character study' film
jokes.punchline: Contrasting 'artsy' with graphic torture porn description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna delusion - framing obvious torture porn as high art. The contrast between 'artsy character study' and 'Slaughterface' is excellent.

jokes.text: Exactly. In fact, the producers of this movie rented 'Saw' and watched it.
jokes.setup: Frank comparing her movie to 'Saw'
jokes.punchline: Confirming they literally just copied Saw, presented as selling point
jokes.explanation: Great low-budget filmmaking satire - they didn't study Saw, they just rented and watched it once. Jenna's pride in this makes it funnier.

jokes.text: On an unrelated matter, does anyone know where I can get intimate bleaching done in Stamford, Connecticut?
jokes.setup: Jenna announcing she's leaving for Connecticut
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate overshare about intimate grooming needs
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna overshare moment. Character-appropriate but relies on shock value rather than clever writing.

jokes.text: That's what your wife told me in the shower this morning.
jokes.setup: Pete saying Jenna can't leave
jokes.punchline: Same 'your wife' joke repeated
jokes.explanation: Repetitive running gag without evolution or cleverness. The exact same joke structure repeated diminishes impact.

jokes.text: Can we order lunch from IKEA?
jokes.setup: Discussion about lunch ordering
jokes.punchline: Suggesting furniture store as food source
jokes.explanation: Mildly absurd suggestion that works because IKEA does serve food. Economy is good but lacks deeper comedy structure.

jokes.text: Okay, we'll have a contest of strength to decide who gets to pick lunch.
jokes.setup: Dispute over lunch choice
jokes.punchline: Medieval solution to workplace disagreement
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation to absurd conflict resolution. Sets up the arm wrestling subplot but isn't particularly memorable on its own.

jokes.text: Well, then I hate you! 'Cause I'm a Pizza Academy of New York man!
jokes.setup: Kenneth mentions son studies at 'New York Pizza Academy'
jokes.punchline: Rival pizza school loyalty creating instant hatred
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - treating pizza education like serious academic rivalry. The instant hatred over school loyalty is perfectly committed.

jokes.text: That's right. You correctly answered my trick question. Now I am bound by Pizza Academy tradition to grant you any request.
jokes.setup: Testing Kenneth about pizza school knowledge
jokes.punchline: Magical oath system for pizza school graduates
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of pizza school absurdity into formal magical tradition. Treats pizza education with fantasy novel seriousness.

jokes.text: According to the transitive property, you just defeated Muammar Qaddafi in arm wrestling.
jokes.setup: Pete wins arm wrestling match
jokes.punchline: Mathematical logic applied to suggest Pete beat dictator
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - using mathematical principle to connect arm wrestling victory to dictator defeat. Perfect nerd humor with political edge.

jokes.text: Help, Paula, here comes the big girl! She's got a clarinet!
jokes.setup: Pete explaining his arm wrestling strength
jokes.punchline: Revealing childhood trauma involving clarinet-wielding girl
jokes.explanation: Good character moment revealing Pete's pathetic childhood. The clarinet detail makes it specific and memorable.

jokes.text: Takeout from Hooters! What? That makes no sense! We'll know they touched it!
jokes.setup: Pete gets to choose lunch as winner
jokes.punchline: Choosing Hooters then realizing the inappropriate implications
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe moment about Hooters takeout logic. Frank's horrified reaction sells the inappropriateness.

jokes.text: Yeah, no, it's just that we're spending taxpayer money saying there's a veterinarian running around Connecticut trying to make a house out of breasts for the Governor to live in.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Connecticut won't give tax breaks
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific bizarre plot details presented matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Incredible escalation of horror movie plot to absurd extremes. The bureaucratic delivery of insane plot points is perfect.

jokes.text: But there's a positive message at the end of it. 'If you're a woman in Connecticut, Slaughterface will kill you.'
jokes.setup: Defending the movie's positive message
jokes.punchline: Message is actually threatening warning to women
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark irony - Jenna thinks death threat is positive message. Perfect character delusion meets horror movie satire.

jokes.text: Large cheese pizza with one slice taken out so I can pretend I'm eating Pac-Man, like my hero Blinky the ghost.
jokes.setup: Describing Tracy's signature pizza order
jokes.punchline: Elaborate Pac-Man fantasy with confused character reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy Jordan absurdity - elaborate food fantasy that shows character's childlike mind. Blinky reference adds extra layer of confusion.

jokes.text: Well, well, well. You found me... after I ordered Thai food and gave you this address.
jokes.setup: Dramatic discovery scene
jokes.punchline: Revealing he basically told them where he was
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that deflates dramatic moment. Perfect Tracy logic - dramatic entrance while admitting he gave himself away.

jokes.text: A copy of 'Black Yachts' magazine and a Rubik's Cube that's been smashed out of anger.
jokes.setup: Kenneth listing Tracy's belongings left behind
jokes.punchline: Absurd magazine title and specific emotional state of damaged puzzle
jokes.explanation: Excellent Tracy character details - 'Black Yachts' is perfect absurd wealth fantasy, smashed Rubik's cube shows his impatience.

jokes.text: I'll get in my bra, and you can throw nails at me! I'll dance for you.
jokes.setup: Liz panicking about TGS being cancelled
jokes.punchline: Desperate offers of increasingly degrading acts
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Liz's desperation. The specific degrading offers reveal her panic about losing her identity without the show.

jokes.text: For the love of God, come back or we'll die!
jokes.setup: Kenneth calling out for Tracy
jokes.punchline: Treating Tracy's return as literal life-or-death situation
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth character moment showing his devotion to Tracy. The religious language fits his character's earnestness.

jokes.text: Jack, can we talk, one ten to another? I'm an eleven, but continue.
jokes.setup: Jenna proposing they talk as equals
jokes.punchline: Jack immediately asserting superiority with higher rating
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for both. Jenna's vanity meets Jack's competitive superiority. The '11' immediately deflates her attempt at equality.

jokes.text: I met that soccer ball once at a no-hands-allowed sex party.
jokes.setup: Jack mentioning soccer ball with moustache from Telemundo
jokes.punchline: Jenna casually mentioning bizarre sexual encounter with inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Peak Jenna absurdity - treating sexual encounter with soccer ball as casual network memory. The 'no-hands-allowed' detail makes it even more bizarre.

jokes.text: $20,000 in first-class flights for the drill,
jokes.setup: Jack listing development costs for the movie
jokes.punchline: Treating drill as passenger requiring expensive flights
jokes.explanation: Great Hollywood excess satire - treating prop drill like A-list celebrity with rider demands. Absurd but believable for industry.

jokes.text: Okay, we just need to hire some of those ugly people who have the paper and change the shapes on it.
jokes.setup: Jack suggesting they need writers
jokes.punchline: Jenna's dismissive, shallow description of writers and writing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna character moment - reducing complex creative work to 'changing shapes on paper.' Her dismissive attitude toward writers is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Writers? No. We'll do the work ourselves. Meet me in my dressing room. I'll get a computer from one of the ugly people. And I'll bring the world's greatest encyclopedia, my mind.
jokes.setup: Jenna suggesting hiring writers
jokes.punchline: Jack's ego-driven refusal combined with Jenna's narcissistic contribution
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing both their egos. Jack's executive arrogance meets Jenna's self-regard as walking encyclopedia.

jokes.text: Hey, is that a to-do list? 'Cause if your wife's on there, I already taken care of her.
jokes.setup: Reggie seeing Pete with a list
jokes.punchline: Third 'your wife' joke in slightly different form
jokes.explanation: Third repetition of the same basic joke with minimal variation. Running gag has become tiresome without evolution.

jokes.text: I just have to work around a PTA meeting at 5:00 that I can't miss 'cause this week I'm snack dad.
jokes.setup: Pete accepting arm wrestling challenge
jokes.punchline: Tough guy moment undercut by domestic dad responsibilities
jokes.explanation: Good character moment contrasting Pete's attempt at machismo with suburban dad reality. 'Snack dad' is perfect specific detail.

jokes.text: Lemon, you look terrible, and I once watched you eat oysters while you had a cold.
jokes.setup: Jack commenting on Liz's appearance
jokes.punchline: Specific disgusting memory as comparison point
jokes.explanation: Great Jack/Liz dynamic - his disgusted concern for her paired with specific gross memory. The oyster detail makes it memorably revolting.

jokes.text: I ended up eating a swordfish dinner at a strip club and Kenneth grabbed a cop's gun and shot a blimp.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining their attempts to think like Tracy
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd actions culminating in blimp shooting
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of absurd Tracy-like behavior. The progression from swordfish at strip club to Kenneth shooting blimps is perfectly insane.

jokes.text: Yes. There was some confusion, and I ended up punching the real Le Var Burton.
jokes.setup: Jack asking if she checked Madame Tussauds wax museum
jokes.punchline: Accidentally assaulting beloved Reading Rainbow host
jokes.explanation: Great absurd escalation - mistaking real person for wax figure and assaulting beloved children's TV host. The casualness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I hope he's not still in heat. He has gotten my top off before.
jokes.setup: Liz going to get Jeremy the lizard
jokes.punchline: Implying sexual relationship with pet lizard
jokes.explanation: Mildly absurd bestiality implication. Works for cringe factor but relies more on shock than cleverness.

jokes.text: Could you use that to motivate Slaughterface sewing the sheriff's mouth to his own anus?
jokes.setup: Jenna mentioning Connecticut's oldest public library
jokes.punchline: Connecting cultural fact to graphic torture scene
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of wholesome Connecticut tourism fact with horrific movie content. The specific grotesque detail sells the absurdity.

jokes.text: Of course. Elegant. Should 'Vaginatorium' be capitalized?
jokes.setup: Jack calling the library connection 'elegant'
jokes.punchline: Jenna treating made-up vulgar word as serious writing concern
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist writing moment - treating ridiculous made-up word with grammatical seriousness. Shows their commitment to terrible movie.

jokes.text: Yeah I am. Last night I had sex with Paula, and neither of us was wearing a Walkman.
jokes.setup: Pete feeling like a new man after arm wrestling success
jokes.punchline: Sad marital milestone involving outdated technology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pete character moment revealing pathetically low bar for marital intimacy. Walkman detail shows how disconnected their relationship has been.

jokes.text: And we're getting appetizers, 'cause David can afford them.
jokes.setup: Stephanie describing dinner with new partner David
jokes.punchline: Emphasizing David's financial superiority through appetizer affordability
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Pete's ex-wife's petty financial digs. Appetizers as luxury item emphasizes their modest circumstances.

jokes.text: Well, of course he can, Mr. Big Shot Pet Photographer! I hope you get sleep at night.
jokes.setup: Pete responding to Stephanie's financial bragging
jokes.punchline: Treating pet photography as corrupt profession deserving guilt
jokes.explanation: Good Pete desperation moment - treating innocent pet photography like moral corruption. Shows his jealousy and financial insecurity.

jokes.text: I never thought I'd feel sorry for a guy with a tattoo of a leprechaun vomiting on a book.
jokes.setup: Frank commenting on Pete's pathetic situation
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd tattoo description that somehow makes Pete sympathetic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific character detail. The leprechaun vomiting on book tattoo is perfectly absurd and anti-intellectual, yet Frank still pities Pete.

jokes.text: I still haven't tried the famous seafood pizza at Sally's in New Haven!
jokes.setup: Being threatened with death in Connecticut forest
jokes.punchline: Focusing on local tourism attraction instead of mortal danger
jokes.explanation: Good horror movie parody - victim's last words being about local food tourism. Specific Connecticut reference adds authenticity.

jokes.text: Let's move on to the scene where Slaughterface centipedes Jenna to the docent of the Danbury Railway Museum.
jokes.setup: Moving to next scene in horror movie
jokes.punchline: Combining Human Centipede reference with specific Connecticut cultural institution
jokes.explanation: Great juxtaposition of horror trope with mundane Connecticut tourism. 'Centipede' as verb plus railway museum docent is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I can never find stuff to watch with my kids. Thank you, Wal-Mart!
jokes.setup: Wal-Mart funding family-friendly version of torture porn
jokes.punchline: Genuine gratitude for wholesome content from inappropriate source
jokes.explanation: Good corporate satire - Wal-Mart executive missing irony of funding family version of horror movie. Sincere gratitude makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You know what your wife told me in the shower this morning? You're a good guy.
jokes.setup: Fourth setup for 'your wife' joke
jokes.punchline: Subverting expectation with actually sweet message
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to running gag - subverts expectation of crude joke with genuine sweetness. Redeems the repetitive setup.

jokes.text: 'Ello! I'm a baby!
jokes.setup: Kenneth answering Tracy's Skype call
jokes.punchline: Bizarre baby voice greeting to lizard
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth character moment showing his childlike relationship with Tracy's pet. The baby voice is appropriately weird.

jokes.text: Merry Christmas! I practice abstinence! Connecticut!
jokes.setup: Filming family-friendly version of horror movie
jokes.punchline: Random wholesome statements replacing horror dialogue
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist family-friendly replacement dialogue. Random wholesome statements show how forced the sanitization is.

jokes.text: I did okay. I have a refrigerator that's just for soda, so...
jokes.setup: Phil being modest about his success
jokes.punchline: Humble-bragging about absurd luxury item
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity cameo moment - false modesty followed by casual mention of ridiculous luxury. Soda refrigerator is perfect excess detail.

jokes.text: But thank you for tying your headshot around a brick and throwing it through my window.
jokes.setup: Jenna asking about her Raymond audition from 15 years ago
jokes.punchline: Revealing extreme method of submission she used
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation revealing Jenna's desperate audition method. The specific visual of headshot-wrapped brick is memorable and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Ha! I beat you! We're getting lunch from IKEA! IKEA! IKEA! IKEA!
jokes.setup: Pete winning arm wrestling match
jokes.punchline: Victory celebration over furniture store lunch, then chanting
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to IKEA lunch with victory celebration. The chanting escalates the absurdity but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: You have been hiding in my apartment for the last 2 weeks? What are you mad about? I'm waiving the $60,000 you owe me in appearance fees.
jokes.setup: Discovering Tracy has been hiding at Liz's
jokes.punchline: Tracy treating hiding as favor worth enormous appearance fee
jokes.explanation: Great Tracy logic - treating inconveniencing Liz as billable service. The specific $60k amount shows his inflated sense of worth.

jokes.text: Did you really think I wouldn't recognize my college futon with its trademark absence of sex stains?
jokes.setup: Liz recognizing her own furniture at Tracy's hideout
jokes.punchline: Self-aware joke about her lack of sexual activity in college
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-deprecating Liz moment. Perfect character humor about her romantic history, with 'trademark absence' being particularly clever phrasing.

jokes.text: So where's the last place you would be if you're out trying to save your show? Home. Life lesson from an unlikely source!
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his hiding logic
jokes.punchline: Tracy being accidentally wise about Liz's priorities
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy wisdom moment. Self-aware about being an unlikely source of insight.

jokes.text: 'TGS' is your whole life. So where's the last place you would be if you you're out trying to save your show? Home. Life lesson from an unlikely source!
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his hiding logic
jokes.punchline: Tracy accidentally delivering genuine wisdom about Liz's life
jokes.explanation: Great character moment where Tracy accidentally provides insight. His awareness that it's an 'unlikely source' shows rare self-awareness.

jokes.text: And yet you still don't have the one I'm thinking of! It's red, it says 'ketchup' on it...
jokes.setup: Tracy complaining about Liz's mustard selection
jokes.punchline: Describing ketchup as type of mustard
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy confusion moment treating ketchup as exotic mustard. Shows his childlike misunderstanding of basic condiments.

jokes.text: As a time saver, I will refer to the two of you as 'Klemon.' I wanted that next level, Klemon. Now, remember, to save time, you two are 'Klemon.'
jokes.setup: Tracy creating portmanteau for Kenneth and Liz
jokes.punchline: Immediately forgetting and re-explaining the time-saving device
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy logic - creating time-saving device then wasting time explaining it twice. The repetition shows his scattered thinking.

jokes.text: Like the time I got stuck in Temple Grandin's hugging machine at the Golden Globes party.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining pressure he feels
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd celebrity encounter involving autism researcher's invention
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist Tracy story combining real person (Temple Grandin) with her actual invention in ridiculous celebrity context.

jokes.text: Sean Penn wanted me to go to Haiti with him, and I'm not strong enough for the pain and the human misery of a 3-hour plane ride with Sean Penn.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining why he had to hide
jokes.punchline: Sean Penn's personality being worse than Haitian disaster
jokes.explanation: Brilliant celebrity satire - Sean Penn's intensity being more unbearable than actual humanitarian crisis. Perfect structure and timing.

jokes.text: Hulk Hogan called you a dirtbag. The NAACP once hired someone to kill you. You wore a penis hat to Princess Diana's funeral!
jokes.setup: Liz reminding Tracy of his terrible past behavior
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of increasingly specific offenses
jokes.explanation: Exceptional escalation of Tracy's offenses from Hulk Hogan insult to NAACP assassination to royal funeral hat incident. Perfect character history.

jokes.text: Because I had worn it to their wedding. It was special to the three of us!
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining the penis hat at Diana's funeral
jokes.punchline: Revealing he wore it to the royal wedding first, making it sentimental
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and callback - making the offensive hat gesture somehow sentimental. The 'three of us' implies friendship with royals.

jokes.text: Go trash a hotel room. Expose yourself to Elmo. Visit O.J. In jail again. Attack the Lincoln Memorial with a hammer.
jokes.setup: Liz telling Tracy how to destroy his reputation
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of increasingly specific terrible acts
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of increasingly specific bad behavior suggestions. The progression from generic (hotel) to specific (Lincoln Memorial hammer) is excellent.

jokes.text: I'm the hero cop and I'm here to say don't do drugs is what I'm here to say! I can't believe that amazing rap is available now on iTunes!
jokes.setup: Family-friendly cop movie with terrible rap
jokes.punchline: Immediately promoting iTunes availability
jokes.explanation: Good satire of bad family entertainment and immediate merchandising. The terrible rap and instant iTunes plug work together.

jokes.text: Vote? For what? Isn't this supposed to be a movie? Again, it doesn't matter. People will just do it, and we get 99 cents a text.
jokes.setup: Confusion about voting in what should be a movie
jokes.punchline: Jack's cynical explanation of text voting revenue scheme
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how the movie has become a text-voting revenue scheme. Jack's cynicism about audience manipulation is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: To vote for Phil Rosenthal, text Phil to 62288. To vote for Liz Lemon, text Liz to 62288.
jokes.setup: Movie somehow becoming a voting competition
jokes.punchline: Reality show voting mechanics in a movie
jokes.explanation: Good absurd escalation of how the movie keeps changing formats. The specific text numbers make it feel authentic.
